next_steps: Perform bronchoalveolar lavage with staining for Strongyloides larvae (O&P) and fungal stains
Perform skin biopsy of the purpuric lesions for pathology and tissue culture
Send serum and CSF for Strongyloides serology and cryptococcal antigen
Perform serial imaging of the abdomen to monitor for worsening ileus or perforation
Initiate empiric ivermectin coverage for suspected hyperinfection syndrome
Perform diagnostic bronchoscopy with bronchoalveolar lavage, including fungal/bacterial cultures and examination for Strongyloides larvae
Perform urgent punch biopsy of the purpuric lesions for histopathology and tissue culture
Initiate empirical therapy with ivermectin for potential Strongyloides hyperinfection
Send specialized blood cultures with prolonged incubation for fastidious organisms
Check serum beta-D-glucan and Aspergillus galactomannan
Obtain follow-up imaging and evaluate for potential infectious complications of the abdominal ileus
Perform stool examination for ova and parasites (specifically looking for rhabditiform larvae)
